name: Preshaw
overview: Preshaw is a very small estate hamlet on high downland north of Corhampton, historically split between the parishes of Upham and Exton, in the City of Winchester district of Hampshire, within the South Downs National Park. It is centred on Preshaw House, a country mansion set in 19th-century parkland with views towards the coast, and a small private chapel (Preshaw Chapel, served seasonally from Upham). There is no village core, shop, pub or school; Preshaw is essentially the estate and a handful of farms and cottages, with everyday amenities in Upham, Bishop's Waltham and Corhampton/Meonstoke.
character: A remote, private estate hamlet of parkland, farmland and a few houses high on the South Downs, with long views and deep rural quiet. The character is defined by the Preshaw estate, downland and woodland rather than any village street.
amenities: No shop, pub or school in Preshaw itself
Preshaw Chapel (private estate chapel, served from Upham in summer)
Primary school, shop, village hall and two pubs in Upham (~3 mi)
Shops, services and pubs in Bishop's Waltham (~4-5 mi)
Corhampton, Meonstoke and the Meon Valley nearby

commutes.notes: Nearest town for shops and services
Via the A272; mainline station to London Waterloo

prices.summary: Preshaw is dominated by the estate and a very small number of farms and houses; the surrounding SO32 South Downs market is firmly premium, with detached country houses commonly £700k-£2m+ and estate properties well above. Open-market sales are very rare.

thingsToDo: Walking the South Downs National Park downland and the Monarch's Way
Exploring the Meon Valley and Corhampton/Meonstoke villages
Country pubs of the Upper Meon Valley
Downland cycling with far-reaching views to the coast
placesToEat: The Brushmakers Arms and The Alma, Upham (~3 mi)
Pubs in Corhampton, Meonstoke and Droxford nearby
Wide choice of pubs and cafes in Bishop's Waltham (~4-5 mi)
pros: Exceptional privacy and downland views in the South Downs National Park
Within reach of Upham and Bishop's Waltham amenities
Outstanding walking and countryside all around
cons: No amenities whatsoever in the hamlet; entirely car-dependent
No mainline station within easy reach; remote lanes
whoItSuits: Buyers seeking extreme rural privacy and downland surroundings within the South Downs National Park, content to drive to Upham and Bishop's Waltham for everyday needs and to Winchester for rail.
